docs/context/BACKUP_STRATEGY.md: covers code backup/branching, not production incident response or data backup. docs/CI_CD_PIPELINE.md: covers CI/CD test pipeline, not production operational runbook. docs/sprints/SPRINT_11_TEST_ISOLATION_AND_SECURITY_VALIDATION.md: covers test database isolation, not launch incident/backup/access control baseline. No docs/ops/ or docs/compliance/ directory exists. OPS_S1_PREFLIGHT.md §3.4 assessment confirmed correct: CI/CD posture is good, but production runbooks, monitoring/alerting, and backup/recovery are documented gaps.

Prompt §SCOPE items 1-7 (lines 123-286) define exact required artifacts with named destination paths (docs/ops/, docs/compliance/) and explicit content requirements. §OUT OF SCOPE (lines 304-314) prohibits: OPS-S1A trust-surface rewrite, backend reasoning/narrative, broad infra replatforming, SOC2/ISO certification, HIPAA/US posture, B2B procurement, full analytics, launch-posture re-decision, substituting docs for unresolved strategic decisions. Scope is entirely documentation artifacts — no backend or analytical code changes.

Prompt scope item 7 (lines 271-286): validation must show (1) required artifacts created, (2) artifact set internally coherent with agreed launch posture, (3) no backend analytical behaviour changed, (4) no unsupported trust claims reintroduced through ops artifacts. Prompt §VALIDATION/EVIDENCE (lines 374-388) requires: exact artifacts created, exact hosting/residency position documented, exact vendor scope, exact privacy-risk/data-flow artifacts, exact open items. Proportionate to docs-dominant scope.

Five explicit STOP conditions (lines 358-370): (1) cannot produce truthful evidence without pretending facts not in evidence, (2) artifacts depend on external unknowns making docs misleading, (3) drift into broad infra/security programme, (4) substituting documentation for unresolved strategic decisions, (5) widening into analytics or backend product work. Condition 1 is directly relevant: hosting region is not repo-enforced; artifact must document this honestly rather than asserting UK guarantees the repo cannot prove.

Prompt §IMPLEMENTATION CONSTRAINTS 'Product logic untouched' (line 334): 'No analytical, reasoning, or results-hierarchy work belongs in this sprint.' All scope items produce docs/* artifacts only. No backend/core/pipeline/, backend/ssot/, backend/scripts/, or frontend analytics code in scope. Intelligence Core (SOP §3) entirely unaffected. Emitted analytical reasoning identical to pre-sprint state.

§OUT OF SCOPE (lines 304-314) is comprehensive. §IMPLEMENTATION CONSTRAINTS (lines 318-338): 'Evidence must be honest', 'Phase 1 baseline only', 'Open items must stay visible', 'Product logic untouched', 'Keep it usable'. STOP condition 3 halts if drift to broad infra/security programme. STOP condition 4 halts if substituting docs for unresolved strategic decisions. The most material drift risk (overclaiming UK hosting) is directly addressed by the prompt's 'Evidence must be honest' constraint and STOP condition 1.

Scope is strictly: new docs under docs/ops/ and docs/compliance/; optional bounded validation artifacts. backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/, backend/scripts/run_work_package.py, backend/scripts/golden_gate_local.py, backend/scripts/update_cursor_status.py — all explicitly excluded by prompt §OUT OF SCOPE. No tooling directories (.codex/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md) in scope.
